title: Wiki
content:
### Enabling the Wiki

Go to **Settings → Features** and check **Wikis**. Wiki is enabled by default for public repos.

### Wiki Pages

Each wiki is a separate Git repository cloned from `https://github.com/user/repo.wiki.git`. Pages use Markdown and are organized hierarchically.

### Sidebar & Footer

- **_Sidebar.md** — Custom navigation sidebar
- **_Footer.md** — Custom footer shown on all pages

Create these files in the wiki repository to customize the layout.

### Editing Wiki Locally

```bash
# Clone the wiki repository
git clone https://github.com/username/repo.wiki.git

# Add or edit pages
# Commit and push to update the wiki
git add .
git commit -m "Update wiki pages"
git push
```

### Wiki as Documentation

Use the wiki for extended documentation, FAQs, design docs, API references, and onboarding guides — separate from the README's quick-start focus. Wikis support revision history and can be reverted if needed.
codeExample:
git clone https://github.com/username/repo.wiki.git
cd repo.wiki
echo "# My Page" > Home.md
git add Home.md
git commit -m "Add Home page"
git push

quiz.question: What is the URL suffix to clone a repo's wiki?
Which file controls the wiki sidebar navigation?
